title: High Throughput Screening with Fluorescent Probe
author: Tjusls China
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6guhbww
materials: Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader

steps.title: Plate Preparation
steps.instruction: Soak the 96-well plates in 75% ethanol and put the container in ultrasonic cleaner for 30min to 1 hour, then use ddH 2 O to wash these plates several times. Put clean plates in drying oven at 55°C. 55 °C

steps.title: Enzyme Dilution
steps.instruction: Dilute the enzyme using its buffer. There we pipet 1 μL protein stock solution in 1mL buffer and mix gently. Then pipet 200 μL protein solution then mix with 12.6mL buffer to reach our aiming concentration(1.51nM in reaction system).

steps.title: Substrate Dilution
steps.instruction: Dilute the substrate(fluorescent probe CDC-1) with DMSO to reach aiming concentration(10.2μM in reaction system).

steps.title: Add Protein Solution to Wells
steps.instruction: Pipet 94 μL protein solution into each well of 96-well plates using multi-channel pipette.

steps.title: Add Compounds and Incubate
steps.instruction: Pipet 1μL compounds from FDA approved drug library into each well except for the first line of the plate. Pipet 1μL 100% DMSO into wells in the first line as negative controls. Then incubate the protein with compounds at room temperature for 5 min.

steps.title: Add Substrate
steps.instruction: Pipet 5 μL substrate into each well of 96-well plates using multi-channel pipette quickly.

steps.title: Set Up Microplate Reader Program
steps.instruction: Set up the program in Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader. Shake for 10 sec at 654 rpm Kinetic Cycle (to read fluorescent intensity each cycle) Fluorescent measure, 25 cycle, 30sec for each cycle

steps.title: Start Reading
steps.instruction: Put the plate in Microplate reader, click Start button.

steps.title: Data Analysis - Initial Velocity
steps.instruction: When the facility ends testing, save data and import it into GraphPad Prism Software. Use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” to calculate the initial velocity of each reaction a.k.a. its slope value.

steps.title: Calculate Residue Activity and Inhibition Ratio
steps.instruction: Compare the values of wells which has added compounds with negative control. Calculate the residue activity(Ra = V r /V 0 *100%) and inhibition ratio(Ir = 1- V r /V 0 *100%). Choose the compounds with Ra<20%, that is to say, Ir>80%, to screen again.

steps.title: Repeat Screening for Selected Compounds
steps.instruction: Repeat step 4-8 to screen again. Only to add chosen compounds and set three same wells as parallel experiments. Collect data and calculate Ir more precisely.

steps.title: Perform Fluorescence Quenching Experiment
steps.instruction: Fluorescence quenching experiment: pipet 94μL protein and 5μL fluorescent substrate and mix them. Let it stand still for 30 mins. Then test its maximum fluorescent intensity(Q1). Pipet 1 μL positive compounds then test again(Q2). Calculate the fluorescence quenching rate Qr = (Q1 – Q2)/Q1 *100%.

steps.title: Identify Inhibitors and Measure Kinetics
steps.instruction: Ascertain the inhibitors, whose Ir is more than 80% while Qr is less than 20%. Then some inhibition kinetic constant can be measured.
